text: PENTAGON BOMBSHELL: Hegseth/Caine briefing reveals Epic Fury stats — 90%+ of Iran's regular naval fleet sunk (~150 ships at bottom of ocean), 13,000+ targets struck, 80% air defense destroyed, 90% weapons factories attacked. Iran declared 'combat ineffective for years.' But US intel estimates ~50% of missile launchers remain intact underground. Satellite imagery shows Iran clearing debris at Khomein + Tabriz South missile bases.
verificationNote: C-SPAN, CBS News confirmed Pentagon briefing. Israel Hayom, Iran International confirmed satellite imagery.

text: HEGSETH IMPEACHMENT: Rep. Yassamin Ansari (D-AZ) — first Iranian-American Democrat in Congress — files 6 articles of impeachment. Charges: unauthorized war, targeting civilians, Signalgate classified info mishandling, obstruction of Congress, abuse of power. 8 co-sponsors. Pentagon: 'another charade.' Dead on arrival in GOP-controlled House but establishes congressional record. Meanwhile 10,000+ US troops deploying — USS Bush CSG + Boxer ARG/11th MEU arriving late April.
verificationNote: Axios, UPI, NOTUS confirmed impeachment. Washington Post, Al Jazeera confirmed troop deployment.

text: CEASEFIRE WATCH: Bloomberg/Fortune report 'in-principle agreement' to extend ceasefire by 2 weeks (expires April 21). BUT US 'has not formally agreed.' Three sticking points: nuclear program (US wants 20yr moratorium, Iran offered 5yr), Hormuz reopening, $270B war compensation. Leavitt: 'We feel good.' Vance: 'I feel very good.' Trump: 'not thinking about extending.' Contradictory signals but momentum building. Pakistan army chief Munir mediating in Tehran.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, Fortune, Axios confirmed ceasefire extension reports. PressTV confirmed Araghchi-Munir meeting.

text: UAE-IRAN CALL: First high-level contact since February — VP Mansour bin Zayed called Parliament Speaker Qalibaf on de-escalation. Gulf states positioning for post-war diplomacy. Meanwhile Iran rejects UAE-backed IMO 'safe maritime corridor' at Legal Committee as 'procedurally invalid.' Baghaei: 'Iran is not blockadable — all means of pressure will fail.' CENTCOM: blockade now 9 tankers turned back. USS Spruance intercepts Iranian-flagged vessel fleeing Bandar Abbas.
verificationNote: Gulf News, Khaleej Times confirmed UAE-Iran call. PressTV confirmed IMO rejection. Military Times confirmed USS Spruance intercept.

text: Day 47 EVE roundup: Pentagon — 150 Iranian ships sunk, 13K targets, 80% air defense destroyed. Hegseth impeachment 6 articles filed. Ceasefire extension 'in-principle' (Bloomberg). UAE-Iran first call since Feb. 10K+ US troops deploying (USS Bush CSG + Boxer ARG). Blockade 9 tankers. S&P 500 record 7,022 — all war losses erased. Nasdaq 24,000+. Lebanon 2,167 killed. 3 paramedics killed Mayfadoun triple-tap. Hezbollah 40+ rockets. Oil Brent $95.72. Gas $4.108 (down 1¢).
verificationNote: Compiled from C-SPAN, CBS News, Axios, Bloomberg, Fortune, Gulf News, Washington Post, Al Jazeera, WAFA, Yahoo Finance, CNN, AAA.

text: MAJOR ESCALATION: IRGC Maj. Gen. Abdollahi on state TV threatens to block ALL shipping in Persian Gulf, Sea of Oman, AND Red Sea. This expands Iran's previous threat from Gulf-only to three waterways — signals potential Houthi reactivation in Yemen.
verificationNote: NBC News, Fox News, Newsweek, Al Jazeera all confirmed the statement.

text: Treasury 'Economic Fury' sanctions hit 24+ entities in Shamkhani oil smuggling network + oil-for-gold Hezbollah financing. State Dept calls it 'upending the shadow fleet.' Sanctions waiver expires April 19 — NOT being renewed. Iran's economic isolation deepening rapidly.
verificationNote: US Treasury press release sb0443 and State Dept statement confirmed.

text: Senate votes: 4th war powers resolution blocked 47-52 (Rand Paul sole GOP for; Fetterman sole Dem against). Sanders Israel arms sales: defeated 40-59 and 36-63 but RECORD 40 Democratic senators voted to block — Kelly (D-AZ) notable flip. 60-day War Powers Act deadline hits April 29.
verificationNote: Roll Call, Haaretz, The Intercept confirmed vote tallies.

text: IDF 98th Division nearing full control of Bint Jbeil — 100+ Hezbollah killed since battle began April 9. 3 Radwan special forces fighters captured. Sgt. Maj. Bianco (30, 188th Armored) killed; 13 wounded. IDF says 'within a few days, there won't be major fights left in Lebanon.'
verificationNote: Jerusalem Post, Times of Israel, Naharnet confirmed.

text: Day 48 AM roundup: Iran threatens Gulf/Oman/Red Sea total shipping blockade (Abdollahi). Treasury 'Economic Fury' sanctions — 24+ Shamkhani entities. Sanctions waiver expires April 19. Senate blocks 4th war powers res (47-52). Record 40 Dems vote to block Israel arms. Rubio: first Israel-Lebanon talks since 1993. IDF soldier KIA Bint Jbeil. 98th Div nearing full control. Hezbollah 30 rockets at Nahariya. NPC suspends petrochemical exports. Trump: 'very close to over.' Vance: 'grand bargain.' Pakistan chief Munir in Tehran. Wang Yi-Araghchi call. WH denies ceasefire extension request. Blockade Day 3: 9-10 tankers. IMF cuts growth to 3.1%. EIA raises Brent forecast to $96. Oil: Brent $94.89 WTI $91.50. Gas: $4.108.
verificationNote: Compiled from NBC News, Fox News, US Treasury, State Dept, Roll Call, Haaretz, The Hill, JPost, ToI, Al Jazeera, CNBC, Rigzone, PressTV, Bloomberg.

text: BREAKING: IDF confirms killing Hezbollah coastal sector commander Ismail Baz in airstrike near Ain Ebel, southern Lebanon. Two associates also killed. Separately, LRB commander Maher Qassem Hamdan eliminated at Shaba Farms/Mt Dov. Two senior commanders taken out in single day as IDF Chief Zamir orders heightened readiness and IAF prepares updated strike packages including Iran contingency.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Iran International, JPost, i24NEWS, JNS.

text: Hezbollah claims 39 military operations in past 24 hours — major uptick. Drone strikes targeted IDF 146th Division logistics base near Nahariya and settlement near Liman barracks. 5 IDF soldiers wounded by rocket fire in Bint Jbeil (1 serious). Tempo of Hezbollah attacks increasing even as diplomatic momentum builds.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Tribune India (Al Jazeera), ANI News, Tempo.co.

text: DIPLOMATIC: Pakistan reports 'major breakthrough' tied to Iran's nuclear programme. Army Chief FM Asim Munir met FM Araghchi in Tehran carrying message from Washington. Pakistani sources say US-Iran could meet in Pakistan for Round 2 next week. Three key sticking points: nuclear moratorium (US wants 20-yr, Iran offered 5-yr), Hormuz reopening, and Iran's $270B compensation demand. Ceasefire expires April 22.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, CNBC, Bloomberg, Pakistan Today report. 'Major breakthrough' characterization comes from Pakistani sources; US has not confirmed.

text: HISTORIC: Trump announces on Truth Social that Netanyahu and Lebanese President Aoun will speak by phone Thursday April 17 — first Israel-Lebanon leader call in 34 years. Follows Rubio-hosted trilateral between Israeli ambassador Leiter and Lebanese ambassador Moawad. Netanyahu separately announced expansion of Lebanon buffer zone with 5 maneuvering divisions and demanded Iran's enrichment capability 'annulled.'
verificationNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, CNBC, Manila Times, JPost, Times of Israel.

text: MARKETS: S&P 500 extends record to 7,033 (+0.14%), Nasdaq up. All Iran war losses fully erased. Rally driven by ceasefire deal hopes + strong bank earnings. Oil stable: Brent $94.89 (+18.9%), WTI $91.50 (+21.2%). Gas $4.09 (down 2¢). IMF cut global growth to 3.1%, warned of recession under severe Hormuz scenario. EIA raised 2026 Brent forecast to $96. Sanctions waiver expires in 3 days (April 19).
verificationNote: PBS, Yahoo Finance, NBC News, AAA confirmed. S&P 500 7,033 per Trading Economics.

text: Day 48 PM roundup: Trump announces historic Netanyahu-Aoun call (1st in 34 yrs). Pakistan 'major breakthrough' on Iran nuclear — Munir-Araghchi meeting in Tehran. IDF kills Hezbollah coastal cmdr Baz + LRB cmdr Hamdan. Hezbollah drones hit 146th Division base Nahariya + Liman. 5 IDF wounded Bint Jbeil. IDF Chief Zamir heightened readiness; IAF preparing Iran strike packages. Netanyahu: 5 divisions in Lebanon buffer zone; enrichment 'annulled.' Blockade Day 3: 10 ships turned back. S&P 7,033 (record). Oil: Brent $94.89 WTI $91.50. Gas: $4.09 (down 2¢). Sanctions waiver April 19.
verificationNote: Compiled from Al Jazeera, CNBC, Iran International, JPost, Israel Hayom, Tribune India, Tempo.co, Trading Economics, PBS, AAA.

text: BREAKING: Trump announces 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire starting 5 PM ET (21:00 GMT). 'First meaningful talks since 1983.' Aoun and Netanyahu invited to White House. CRITICAL CAVEAT: Israel says IDF will NOT withdraw from southern Lebanon — will maintain 'freedom of action' against Hezbollah. No Hezbollah confirmation. Senior Israeli official tells Haaretz: 'There is no ceasefire in Lebanon.' IDF to continue operations to Litani River.
verificationNote: Axios, PBS, Bloomberg, NBC News, Haaretz confirmed ceasefire announcement and IDF non-withdrawal.

text: BLOCKADE GOES GLOBAL: Gen. Caine (CJCS) announces US Navy will now intercept any Iranian-flagged vessel ANYWHERE IN THE WORLD — including Pacific AOR under Adm. Paparo. 13 ships turned back in 3 days. No boardings yet. Contraband includes weapons, nuclear materials, crude/refined oil, iron, steel, aluminum. Hegseth: US 'locked and loaded' to attack Iran's electrical power grid if talks fail. 'Reloading with more power than ever before.'
verificationNote: Breaking Defense, Stars and Stripes, Military.com, Bloomberg confirmed global blockade expansion and 13 ships.

text: QASMIYEH BRIDGE DESTROYED: Israeli warplanes hit the last remaining bridge over the Litani River with 2 consecutive strikes. ~10% of Lebanon's territory now fully severed from the rest of the country. Lebanese army evacuated checkpoint. Separately, 4 paramedics killed in Mayfadoun triple-tap — 3 ambulance teams hit in succession. 91+ Lebanese medical workers killed since March 2. Ceasefire announced hours later — pattern of escalation before de-escalation.
verificationNote: Xinhua, CBC News, Naharnet confirmed bridge destruction. Al Jazeera, Newsweek confirmed paramedic killings.

text: IRAN THREATS: Mohsen Rezaei warns US warships in Strait of Hormuz are 'under our missile launchers' and threatens to 'sink them all.' Claims launchers aimed at USS Abraham Lincoln. Meanwhile Pakistan Army Chief Munir meets Iranian Parliament Speaker — Pakistani sources say visit 'significantly contributed to narrowing differences.' White House 'optimistic' about deal. Round 2 of US-Iran talks likely in Islamabad next week. Ceasefire expires April 22.
verificationNote: Press TV confirmed Rezaei statement. Bloomberg, Al Jazeera confirmed Pakistan mediation progress.

text: Day 48 EVE roundup: CEASEFIRE — Trump announces 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire (5 PM ET). Israel won't withdraw from south Lebanon. No Hezbollah confirmation. BLOCKADE GLOBAL — US Navy to intercept Iranian ships worldwide. 13 ships turned back. Hegseth threatens Iran's electrical grid. INFRASTRUCTURE — Qasmiyeh Bridge destroyed, last Litani crossing. 4 paramedics killed Mayfadoun. IRAN — Rezaei threatens to sink US warships. Pakistan 'narrowing differences.' MARKETS — S&P pulls back 0.3% from record. Oil: Brent $94.89 WTI $91.29. Gas: $4.093. Sanctions waiver expires April 19.
verificationNote: Compiled from Axios, PBS, Bloomberg, Breaking Defense, Xinhua, CBC, Newsweek, Press TV, Al Jazeera, AAA.
